rejected_candidates.title: Arbitrary External Call in updateSettings Allows Complete Contract Takeover
rejected_candidates.reason: `updateSettings` is a privileged admin mechanism, not an untrusted user entrypoint: owner can choose any target, and non-owner callers are restricted to a preconfigured lower-admin contract target.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Non-Reentrant Protection on updateSettings
rejected_candidates.reason: The reviewed code shows only an admin-gated maintenance path here; no concrete unprivileged reentrancy primitive or exploitable intermediate state was identified.

rejected_candidates.title: Flash Loan Allows Arbitrary External Calls to Any Target
rejected_candidates.reason: This is the intended flash-loan callback design; the function snapshots balances before the callback and requires both ether and underlying balances to be restored afterward.

rejected_candidates.title: No Validation of Price Feed Return Values
rejected_candidates.reason: `_totalDeposit` already rejects zero rate and zero precision; remaining stale/manipulated oracle concerns are generic trust assumptions, not a code-specific bug demonstrated here.

rejected_candidates.title: Token Price Calculation Vulnerable to Flash Loan Manipulation
rejected_candidates.reason: `flashBorrow` snapshots `_flTotalAssetSupply` for the duration of the flash loan, specifically preventing intra-transaction supply/price distortion from the borrowed assets.

rejected_candidates.title: No Deadline Parameter in Borrow and Margin Trade Functions
rejected_candidates.reason: Missing deadlines are a generic UX/MEV consideration, not a concrete protocol vulnerability in this code.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked Return Value in Token TransferFrom
rejected_candidates.reason: `_callOptionalReturn` requires the low-level call to succeed and also decodes and requires the returned boolean when return data is present.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential Integer Overflow in Interest Calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: The arithmetic in the cited interest calculation uses `SafeMath`; overflows revert rather than silently wrapping.

rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded Gas Token Addresses Could Become Invalid
rejected_candidates.reason: This is configuration brittleness, but it does not by itself create a realistic exploit or protocol-level loss scenario.

rejected_candidates.title: Miner Front-Running Risk Due to Missing Access Control on Pause
rejected_candidates.reason: The cited code only reads pause flags from storage; no public pause-setting function or missing access control was shown in the reviewed source.

rejected_candidates.title: Floating Pragma Solidity Version
rejected_candidates.reason: The pragma is pinned to `0.5.17`, and compiler pinning/version choice is not a reportable vulnerability here.
